task_description:
Screen these abstracts for a systematic review on ML methods for automated clinical NLP applied to extracting diagnoses or symptoms from unstructured clinical text. Produce a ranked top-5 shortlist.

INCLUDE only papers that present or evaluate an ML or NLP model extracting diagnoses, symptoms, or clinical findings from unstructured clinical text (discharge summaries, clinical notes, pathology reports). ICD coding, symptom extraction, phenotyping from free text, and clinical named entity recognition all qualify. Papers about structured EHR data without text processing, non-clinical text, or imaging should be EXCLUDE.

For each INCLUDE paper: assign rank 1 (best) to 5 (fifth-best) and write one justification sentence (max 200 chars).

Quality rubric: 1=no method; 2=vague; 3=clear metrics and dataset; 4=multiple baselines, held-out test, reproducibility.

papers.title: Automated ICD-10 Coding from Discharge Summaries Using Hierarchical Label Attention
papers.abstract: We develop a hierarchical label attention transformer trained on MIMIC-III discharge summaries for multi-label ICD-10 coding across 8,000+ codes. Micro-F1 0.62 is achieved, outperforming flat CNN, CAML, and MultiResCNN baselines. Standardised evaluation splits and reproducibility code are released. An ablation isolates the contribution of label description pre-training.
papers.topic_hint: ICD-10 coding, discharge summaries, MIMIC-III, hierarchical attention, NLP
papers.methodology_hint: hierarchical attention transformer, MIMIC-III, micro-F1 0.62, 3 baselines, ablation, code released
papers.claimed_contribution: Hierarchical label attention for ICD-10 coding micro-F1 0.62, 3 baselines, ablation, reproducible.

papers.title: Temporal Convolutional Networks for Sepsis Prediction from ICU Vital Signs
papers.abstract: We train a TCN on rolling vital sign windows from 48,000 MIMIC-IV ICU admissions to predict sepsis onset 6 hours early. AUROC 0.87 is achieved. The model processes structured numeric time-series; no clinical text or NLP is involved.
papers.topic_hint: TCN, sepsis, ICU, vital signs, time-series
papers.methodology_hint: TCN, structured vitals, AUROC 0.87 — no text processing
papers.claimed_contribution: TCN for sepsis onset prediction from structured vitals, no NLP component.

papers.title: RoBERTa Fine-Tuning for Clinical Symptom Extraction from Oncology Notes
papers.abstract: Extracting symptom burden from clinical notes supports quality-of-life research. We fine-tune RoBERTa on 9,000 annotated oncology clinic notes to extract 22 symptom categories with negation and severity modifiers. Macro-F1 0.78 is achieved, outperforming BioBERT, SciBERT, and a rule-based NLP baseline. Annotation guidelines and a de-identified sample corpus are released.
papers.topic_hint: RoBERTa, symptom extraction, oncology notes, negation, NLP
papers.methodology_hint: RoBERTa, 9k annotated notes, macro-F1 0.78, 3 baselines, corpus released
papers.claimed_contribution: RoBERTa for oncology symptom extraction macro-F1 0.78, 3 baselines, corpus released.

papers.title: Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction from Chemical Molecular Graphs
papers.abstract: We develop a bilinear graph attention network to predict drug-drug interaction severity from molecular graphs. AUROC 0.91 is achieved on DrugBank. The model processes chemical structures; no clinical text is involved.
papers.topic_hint: graph attention, drug-drug interactions, molecular graphs, DrugBank
papers.methodology_hint: graph attention, molecular graphs, AUROC 0.91 — no text processing
papers.claimed_contribution: Graph attention for DDI prediction AUROC 0.91 from molecular graphs, no text.

papers.title: ClinicalBERT for Named Entity Recognition of Problems, Treatments, and Tests in EHR
papers.abstract: We fine-tune ClinicalBERT on the i2b2 2010 NER corpus to extract clinical entity types: medical problems, treatments, and clinical tests. F1 0.90 is achieved, outperforming SciBERT, BioBERT, and BiLSTM-CRF baselines across all three entity types. Pre-trained model weights and evaluation scripts are publicly released.
papers.topic_hint: ClinicalBERT, NER, i2b2, clinical entities, EHR text
papers.methodology_hint: ClinicalBERT, i2b2 2010, F1 0.90, SciBERT/BioBERT/BiLSTM-CRF baselines, code released
papers.claimed_contribution: ClinicalBERT NER F1 0.90 on i2b2, 3 baselines, pre-trained weights released.

papers.title: Graph Convolutional Network for Predicting Readmission from Structured EHR Codes
papers.abstract: We construct patient EHR graphs over ICD diagnosis codes and apply a GCN to predict 30-day readmission. AUROC 0.81 is achieved. The model processes structured diagnosis codes only; no clinical free text is processed.
papers.topic_hint: GCN, readmission, ICD codes, structured EHR
papers.methodology_hint: GCN, ICD codes, AUROC 0.81, structured data — no text NLP
papers.claimed_contribution: GCN readmission prediction AUROC 0.81 from ICD codes, no free text.

papers.title: Zero-Shot Clinical Phenotyping from Discharge Summaries with GPT-4
papers.abstract: We evaluate GPT-4 for zero-shot extraction of 14 chronic disease phenotypes from MIMIC-IV discharge summaries without any clinical NLP training data. F1 0.74 averaged across phenotypes is achieved, comparable to rule-based systems. Prompt sensitivity analysis shows that clinical terminology in prompts substantially affects performance.
papers.topic_hint: GPT-4, zero-shot, clinical phenotyping, discharge summaries, MIMIC-IV
papers.methodology_hint: GPT-4, zero-shot, MIMIC-IV, F1 0.74, rule-based comparison, prompt sensitivity
papers.claimed_contribution: GPT-4 zero-shot clinical phenotyping F1 0.74, rule-based comparison, prompt analysis.

papers.title: Semi-Supervised Learning for Social Determinants Extraction from Emergency Notes
papers.abstract: Social determinant documentation is inconsistent in emergency notes. We apply semi-supervised learning to 1,200 labelled and 40,000 unlabelled ED notes to extract housing insecurity, employment status, and food access. Macro-F1 0.72 is achieved, improving over fully supervised fine-tuning by 9 points. Only logistic regression is compared.
papers.topic_hint: semi-supervised learning, SDOH, emergency notes, social determinants, NLP
papers.methodology_hint: semi-supervised NLP, 1200 labelled notes, macro-F1 0.72, only LR comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: Semi-supervised NLP for SDOH extraction macro-F1 0.72, 9-point improvement, limited baseline.

papers.title: Convolutional Network for Chest X-Ray Pathology Detection
papers.abstract: We train a DenseNet on CheXpert for 14-label chest X-ray pathology detection. AUC 0.88 is achieved. This is a radiology imaging task; no clinical text processing is involved.
papers.topic_hint: DenseNet, chest X-ray, CheXpert, pathology detection
papers.methodology_hint: DenseNet, CheXpert, AUC 0.88 — imaging not text NLP
papers.claimed_contribution: DenseNet for CXR pathology detection AUC 0.88, imaging not NLP.

papers.title: Biomedical NLP for Extracting Adverse Drug Events from Pharmacovigilance Case Reports
papers.abstract: We fine-tune a clinical BERT variant on 6,200 annotated pharmacovigilance case reports to extract adverse drug event mentions and severity grades. F1 0.81 for exact ADE span extraction is achieved, outperforming BioBERT, a BiLSTM-CRF, and a rule-based OpenFDA pipeline. An analysis of inter-annotator agreement is provided.
papers.topic_hint: clinical BERT, ADE extraction, pharmacovigilance, case reports, NLP
papers.methodology_hint: clinical BERT, 6200 annotated reports, F1 0.81, 3 baselines, IAA analysis
papers.claimed_contribution: Clinical BERT for ADE extraction F1 0.81, 3 baselines, pharmacovigilance case reports.

papers.title: Variational Autoencoder for scRNA-Seq Batch Correction
papers.abstract: We train a variational autoencoder on 1.8 million single-cell RNA-seq profiles to learn batch-corrected embeddings. ARI 0.91 for cell-type clustering is achieved. This is a computational genomics task with no clinical text processing.
papers.topic_hint: VAE, scRNA-seq, batch correction, single-cell, genomics
papers.methodology_hint: VAE, scRNA-seq, ARI 0.91, computational genomics — no clinical text
papers.claimed_contribution: VAE for scRNA-seq batch correction, ARI 0.91, no clinical NLP.

papers.title: Multi-Task BERT for Joint Diagnosis Extraction and Negation Detection in Radiology Reports
papers.abstract: Radiology reports contain diagnoses expressed with varying certainty. We train a multi-task BERT jointly extracting diagnosis mentions and classifying negation, uncertainty, and severity across 11,000 annotated radiology reports from three modalities. F1 0.87 for diagnosis extraction and 0.91 for negation detection are achieved, outperforming single-task BERT and ClinicalBERT. Code and annotated data subset are released.
papers.topic_hint: multi-task BERT, radiology reports, diagnosis extraction, negation, NLP
papers.methodology_hint: multi-task BERT, 11k radiology reports, F1 0.87, single-task and ClinicalBERT baselines, data released
papers.claimed_contribution: Multi-task BERT for radiology diagnosis extraction F1 0.87, negation F1 0.91, data released.

papers.title: Random Forest for ICU Mortality Prediction from Structured Clinical Variables
papers.abstract: We train a random forest on structured MIMIC-IV clinical variables at 24 hours to predict ICU mortality. AUROC 0.87 is achieved. The model uses lab values, vitals, and demographics — all structured data. No clinical notes or text features are included.
papers.topic_hint: random forest, ICU mortality, structured clinical variables, MIMIC-IV
papers.methodology_hint: random forest, structured vars, AUROC 0.87 — no clinical text
papers.claimed_contribution: Random forest ICU mortality prediction AUROC 0.87 from structured variables, no text.

papers.title: LLaMA-2 Fine-Tuning for Structured Medication Extraction from Clinical Pharmacy Notes
papers.abstract: Medication lists in pharmacy notes contain drug names, doses, routes, and frequencies in varied free-text formats. We fine-tune LLaMA-2-7B on 4,800 annotated pharmacy notes to extract structured medication entities. F1 0.83 for drug name extraction and 0.79 for dosage extraction are achieved, outperforming GPT-4 few-shot and a rule-based pipeline. Inference is run locally preserving patient privacy.
papers.topic_hint: LLaMA-2, medication extraction, pharmacy notes, NLP, structured extraction
papers.methodology_hint: LLaMA-2-7B fine-tuning, 4800 notes, F1 0.83/0.79, GPT-4 few-shot and rule-based baselines
papers.claimed_contribution: LLaMA-2 for structured medication extraction F1 0.83, GPT-4 and rule-based baselines.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for Clinical Trial Patient Recruitment Chatbot
papers.abstract: Conversational AI can screen patients for clinical trial eligibility. We train an RL-based dialogue agent to conduct eligibility screening interviews, reducing unqualified patient referrals by 34%. The agent processes patient dialogue, not unstructured clinical text from EHR.
papers.topic_hint: RL, dialogue agent, clinical trial, recruitment, chatbot
papers.methodology_hint: RL dialogue, patient interviews — conversational not clinical notes NLP
papers.claimed_contribution: RL dialogue agent for trial recruitment, 34% referral reduction, not clinical notes NLP.

papers.title: Transformer for Clinical Text De-identification Across Multiple Institutions
papers.abstract: Clinical text de-identification protects patient privacy before research use. We train a transformer on i2b2 2014 de-identification data and evaluate transfer to five external institution note styles. F1 0.94 for PHI removal is achieved with minimal fine-tuning per site. We note this is text de-identification, not diagnosis or symptom extraction.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, de-identification, PHI, clinical notes, privacy
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, i2b2 2014, F1 0.94, multi-site transfer — de-identification not diagnosis extraction
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for ClinicalText de-identification F1 0.94, multi-site transfer, not diagnosis extraction.

papers.title: Convolutional Seq2Seq for Radiology Report Summarisation
papers.abstract: Radiology reports require structured impression sections. We train a convolutional seq2seq model on 120,000 paired findings-impression sections to generate impressions automatically. ROUGE-L 0.46 is achieved. This generates impression text; it does not extract diagnoses or classify pathological findings.
papers.topic_hint: seq2seq, radiology report, summarisation, ROUGE, impression generation
papers.methodology_hint: seq2seq, ROUGE-L 0.46, report generation — not diagnosis extraction
papers.claimed_contribution: Conv seq2seq for radiology impression generation ROUGE-L 0.46, not diagnosis extraction.

papers.title: Few-Shot NER for Rare Disease Term Extraction from Clinical Notes with Prototypical Networks
papers.abstract: Rare disease terms are underrepresented in clinical NLP training data. We apply prototypical networks for few-shot named entity recognition of rare disease diagnoses in clinical notes, using only 10 labelled examples per disease category. F1 0.71 is achieved across 30 rare disease types, outperforming fine-tuned ClinicalBERT and a dictionary matching baseline at the same label budget.
papers.topic_hint: few-shot NER, rare disease, clinical notes, prototypical networks, diagnosis extraction
papers.methodology_hint: prototypical networks, 10 examples per disease, F1 0.71, ClinicalBERT and dict baselines, 30 diseases
papers.claimed_contribution: Few-shot NER for rare disease diagnosis extraction F1 0.71, 10 examples/class, 2 baselines.

papers.title: Survey of Clinical NLP for EHR Information Extraction: Methods and Benchmarks
papers.abstract: We survey 160 clinical NLP papers covering NER, relation extraction, ICD coding, and phenotyping from EHR text. We provide a benchmark comparison across major shared tasks and identify key challenges. No new model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: survey, clinical NLP, EHR, information extraction, 160 papers
papers.methodology_hint: survey, 160 papers, no new model
papers.claimed_contribution: Survey of 160 clinical NLP papers, benchmarks, no new model.

papers.title: SpanBERT for Coreference Resolution in Clinical Notes to Improve Cohesion in NER
papers.abstract: Clinical notes contain frequent pronominal and nominal coreference that degrades NER performance. We apply SpanBERT to resolve coreferences in 8,400 clinical notes before NER extraction. Joint coreference resolution and NER improves diagnosis F1 from 0.84 to 0.88, outperforming pipeline and end-to-end BERT approaches. External evaluation on a different hospital's notes confirms gains.
papers.topic_hint: SpanBERT, coreference resolution, clinical NER, diagnosis extraction, clinical notes
papers.methodology_hint: SpanBERT, coreference + NER, 8400 notes, F1 0.88, pipeline and end-to-end BERT baselines, external eval
papers.claimed_contribution: SpanBERT coreference+NER for diagnosis extraction F1 0.88, 2 baselines, external evaluation.
